name: Supertrend KAMA Double Adaptive Trend
edge_hypothesis: The strongest meta-rule in the system (rule-adaptive-over-static, brain U19) states: adaptive indicators produce all-regime ACCEPTs while static indicators top out at PF=1.712 and need regime gates. Both Supertrend and KAMA are adaptive — Supertrend adapts its trailing stop level dynamically based on ATR, KAMA adapts its smoothing speed based on market noise (efficiency ratio). They are INDEPENDENTLY adaptive through DIFFERENT mechanisms: Supertrend via volatility-adjusted levels, KAMA via noise-adjusted speed. This spec tests what happens when two independent adaptive systems must AGREE before entering. Supertrend direction flip (SUPERTd crossing 0) provides the cross-based entry trigger — proven in the 2nd-ranked strategy (PF=1.921, 85 trades). KAMA direction confirmation (close vs KAMA) adds an independent adaptive filter. The existing Supertrend champion uses ADX_14 > 20 (static filter). This spec replaces the static filter with an adaptive one. If PF improves: adaptive+adaptive > adaptive+static. If PF drops: KAMA agreement removes valid entries (too correlated with Supertrend). Either result teaches us about adaptive indicator correlation.

variants.name: st_kama_v1_8to1
variants.description: Core thesis: Supertrend direction flip + KAMA directional confirmation. 8:1 R:R. SUPERTd crossing above 0 (bullish flip) AND close > KAMA (KAMA confirms bullish). Tests adaptive+adaptive vs the existing adaptive+static(ADX) architecture. Uses same R:R as Supertrend champion for direct comparison.

components.notes: Supertrend direction — ATR-adaptive trailing stop. Direction flip (crosses 0) is a proven cross-based entry trigger. 2nd-ranked system strategy at PF=1.921.
Kaufman Adaptive MA — speed adapts to market noise via efficiency ratio. Close vs KAMA confirms adaptive trend direction. Replaces static ADX filter.
ATR-based position sizing and R:R framework.

variants.name: st_kama_chop_v2_8to1
variants.description: Adds CHOP < 50 anti-chop filter. Tests whether explicitly excluding extreme ranges (CHOP > 50) improves Supertrend+KAMA entries. Hypothesis: two adaptive indicators may still fire false signals during extreme chop — the CHOP filter provides a simple regime gate. If PF improves: even adaptive indicators benefit from explicit chop removal. If PF drops: KAMA already handles chop detection implicitly through speed adaptation.

components.notes: Anti-chop gate. < 50 = market not excessively choppy. Tests if adaptive indicators need explicit chop protection.

variants.name: st_kama_v3_tight_10to1
variants.description: Champion-geometry variant: 0.75 ATR stop, 10 ATR TP (same parameters that made Vortex v3a the #1 champion at PF=2.034). Tests whether the tight-stop-wide-TP geometry that maximizes Vortex also maximizes Supertrend+KAMA. If PF > 2.0: this geometry is universally optimal for cross-based adaptive strategies. If PF drops: tight stops require the Vortex reversal exit mechanism that this strategy doesn't have.

components.notes: Supertrend direction flip with champion-tight stop geometry.
KAMA adaptive filter with tight-stop geometry test.
0.75x ATR stop / 7.5x ATR TP — champion geometry from Vortex v3a.

indicator_roles.baseline: SUPERTd_7_3.0 — Supertrend direction indicator. ATR-adaptive trailing stop; direction flip is a proven high-quality cross-based entry trigger.
indicator_roles.confirmation: KAMA_10_2_30 — Kaufman Adaptive MA. Speed adjusts to market noise. Replaces static ADX filter with adaptive trend confirmation.
indicator_roles.volume_volatility: ATR_14 — position sizing and R:R framework.
